MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar May-19-2006 Off Calendar

MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO REQUEST, DEMAND INSPECTION, PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS
Off Calendar May-03-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Albert G. Stoll, Jr., Esq., for Plaintiff/Moving party
Eugenia S. Chern, Esq., for Defendant/Opposing party

Ruling:
Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiff Marie Nicholas's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Request For Production Of Documents is granted in part and denied in part. The motion is denied as to Nos. 1-5, 7-9, 18, 19, 22, and 27-30 as a redaction log has been provided. Defendant is ordered to provide a verification that no additional documents have been withheld pursuant to a claim of privilege
The motion is denied as to Nos. 11, 15, 16 as the responses are adequate.
The motion is granted as to Nos. 6, 12, 13, 14, 25, and 26 as the objections are ordered stricken. The responses are otherwise adequate.
The motion is denied as to Nos. 17 and 21 as they are irrelevant and protected by the right to privacy.
All requests for sanctions are denied.

PARTIES TO MEET AND CONFER REGARDING NO. 11. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT

Amended Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Responses To Request For Production Of Documents & For Award Of Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Apr-27-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearance:
Albert Stoll, Esq., for Plaintiff/Moving party
Eugenia S. Chern, Esq., for Defendant
Ruling:
Plaintiff's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Request For Production Of Documents did not make it onto this calendar due to it having been ordered off calendar in the Law and Motion Department on April 20, 2006. However, the court will order the motion continued to May 3, 2006 at 9 a.m. in this department. Counsel shall lodge courtesy copies of the moving and opposing papers by April 28, 2006. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT

Ntc Of Motion To Compel
Off Calendar Apr-27-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Albert Stoll, Esq., for Plaintiff/Moving party
Eugenia S. Chern, Esq., for Defendant
Ruling:
Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiff's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatory is granted. While the information may fall within the area of privacy, the location of witnesses cannot be maintained in secrecy. Further response shall be made within 10 days of notice of this order. All requests for sanctions are denied.COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT

Off Calendar Jan-25-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: NO APPEARANCES. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Defendant's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Inspection Demands is Granted. No opposition has been filed. Plaintiff's failure to produce documents and to respond fully to form interrogatories was a misuse of the discovery process. Plaintiff is ordered to respond further and to produce documents within 15 days of notice of this order. Sanctions are awarded in the amount of $876.30, to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT
